remember: Students will be able to recall the names and functions of major international organisations such as the United Nations and its agencies, the European Union, the Commonwealth, and the Non-Aligned Movement.
Students will be able to identify the main principles and objectives of international organisations.
understand: Students will be able to understand the structure and functioning of international organisations, including the role of the General Assembly, the Security Council, and the Secretariat in the United Nations.
Students will be able to comprehend the decision-making processes and mechanisms of international organisations.
apply: Students will be able to apply the concepts of international cooperation and global governance to real-world scenarios.
Students will be able to evaluate the effectiveness of international organisations in promoting peace, security, and development.
analyze: Students will be able to analyze the strengths and weaknesses of international organisations in addressing global challenges.
Students will be able to compare and contrast the roles and functions of different international organisations.

ncert_objectives: Students will be able to explain the significance of international organisations in promoting global cooperation and governance.
Students will be able to describe the role of international organisations in maintaining international peace and security.
Students will be able to discuss the impact of international organisations on the lives of individuals and communities.
board_exam_objectives: Students will be able to identify the main features and functions of international organisations as per the syllabus.
Students will be able to explain the significance of international cooperation and global governance in the context of the chapter.
Students will be able to analyze the strengths and weaknesses of international organisations in addressing global challenges, as per the syllabus.
